name: Title 3
text:
CartTruth
Pitch
textPreview: CartTruth | Pitch

name: Subtitle 4
text: Final-checkout price verification for Daraz and beyond.
textPreview: Final-checkout price verification for Daraz and beyond.

name: Rounded Rectangle 2
text: 8-minute pitch
textPreview: 8-minute pitch

text:
0:00-0:25
Good morning everyone. We are presenting CartTruth, a final-checkout price verification platform. Our core idea is simple: product pages make a promise, but checkout tells the truth. When a customer sees a product price online, that is not always the final amount they pay. Delivery charges, platform fees, taxes, vouchers, location, and account-specific conditions can all change the final total. CartTruth helps verify that final checkout price safely, without placing an order.

name: Title 2
text: Checkout is where price trust breaks
textPreview: Checkout is where price trust breaks

name: Content Placeholder 9
text: abandon when extra shipping, tax, or fees are too high. Baymard.
textPreview: abandon when extra shipping, tax, or fees are too high. Baymard.

name: Content Placeholder 10
text: source of truth: the final payable checkout total.
textPreview: source of truth: the final payable checkout total.

name: Content Placeholder 11
text: business ecommerce sales across 45 economies in 2024. UNCTAD.
textPreview: business ecommerce sales across 45 economies in 2024. UNCTAD.

name: Content Placeholder 15
text: Product pages show the promise. Checkout reveals the final payable total; that is where trust, disputes, and abandonment happen.
textPreview: Product pages show the promise. Checkout reveals the final payable total; that is where trust, disputes, and abandonment happen.

name: Footer Placeholder 6
text: CartTruth pitch
textPreview: CartTruth pitch

text:
0:25-1:10
The problem we are addressing is checkout price uncertainty. For shoppers, it causes frustration. For sellers and support teams, it creates confusion when the product page shows one number but checkout reveals another. That final payable amount is the number customers remember. Baymard research identifies extra costs like shipping, taxes, and fees as a leading checkout abandonment reason. UNCTAD shows the scale of ecommerce itself: business ecommerce sales across 45 economies reached 28 trillion dollars in 2024.

name: Footer Placeholder 3
text: CartTruth pitch
textPreview: CartTruth pitch

name: Google Shape;533;p58
text: Manual checks and product-page scrapers miss the real price
textPreview: Manual checks and product-page scrapers miss the real price

name: Google Shape;534;p58
text:
Slow and inconsistent
People open the site, log in, add products, check totals, take screenshots, and repeat it across items.
textPreview: Slow and inconsistent | People open the site, log in, add products, check totals, take screenshots, and repeat it across items.

name: Content Placeholder 1
text:
Wrong viewpoint
Hosted server traffic can be blocked, incomplete, or different from what a real shopper sees locally.
textPreview: Wrong viewpoint | Hosted server traffic can be blocked, incomplete, or different from what a real shopper sees locally.

name: Content Placeholder 2
text:
Stops too early
Catalog scrapers see product-page prices, but not delivery, fees, vouchers, session state, or checkout rules.
textPreview: Stops too early | Catalog scrapers see product-page prices, but not delivery, fees, vouchers, session state, or checkout rules.

name: Content Placeholder 15
text: Manual
textPreview: Manual

name: Content Placeholder 15
text: Scrapers
textPreview: Scrapers

name: Content Placeholder 15
text: Network
textPreview: Network

text:
1:10-1:50
Today many teams solve this manually. Someone opens the marketplace, logs in, adds the item, checks the total, takes screenshots, and repeats the work. Basic scrapers also fail because they usually stop at the product page. The real price appears inside checkout after account session, delivery region, vouchers, and fees are applied. There is also a network issue: ecommerce systems may react differently to hosted traffic than to a normal shopper route.

name: Footer Placeholder 1
text: CartTruth pitch
textPreview: CartTruth pitch

textPreview: CartTruth acts like a careful buyer, then stops

textPreview: The user saves a Daraz product URL and CartTruth reads the observed product-page price.

name: Content Placeholder 10

textPreview: Keep proof

name: Google Shape;534;p58
text: A user-directed checkout verifier for Daraz.lk: save a link, use an isolated session, run a Buy Now check, compare product-page price with checkout total, and keep evidence.
textPreview: A user-directed checkout verifier for Daraz.lk: save a link, use an isolated session, run a Buy Now check, compare product-page price wit...

text:
1:50-2:40
CartTruth solves this by acting like a careful buyer, but stopping before purchase. The user signs in with Google, saves a Daraz product link, and connects their own Daraz session using an isolated server-side browser. CartTruth then runs a Buy Now checkout check, reads the checkout breakdown, captures evidence, and stops before any order is placed. Our current MVP supports Daraz.lk, but the system is designed around adapters so the model can expand.

name: Footer Placeholder 4
text: CartTruth pitch
textPreview: CartTruth pitch

name: Google Shape;533;p58
text: Demo: verify checkout safely
textPreview: Demo: verify checkout safely

name: Google Shape;534;p58
text:
Show the live path
Dashboard -> saved product -> queued check -> checkout breakdown -> evidence artifact.
textPreview: Show the live path | Dashboard -> saved product -> queued check -> checkout breakdown -> evidence artifact.

name: Google Shape;534;p58
text:
Backup if the live flow stalls
Open a recent completed run while the new checkout job continues in the background.
textPreview: Backup if the live flow stalls | Open a recent completed run while the new checkout job continues in the background.

name: Picture 3
alt: CartTruth dashboard showing a saved Daraz product and checkout breakdown

text:
2:40-4:40
Now I will show the working product. First, this is the CartTruth dashboard where we save Daraz product links. CartTruth reads the product-page price, but that is only the starting point. Next I will show a checkout check. The job opens the Daraz flow using the saved session and returns the run result. Here you can see product price, delivery or platform charges, vouchers if available, and the final total. CartTruth also stores evidence. If the live check takes too long or needs verification, I will open a recent completed run immediately.

name: Footer Placeholder 5
text: CartTruth pitch
textPreview: CartTruth pitch

name: Title 2
text: Safety and evidence make the result usable
textPreview: Safety and evidence make the result usable

name: Content Placeholder 9
text:
Never purchase
Network guards block order or payment finalization; unsafe purchase labels are refused.
textPreview: Never purchase | Network guards block order or payment finalization; unsafe purchase labels are refused.

name: Content Placeholder 10
text:
Human verification
OTP, captcha, login, and verification become explicit needs_user_action states.
textPreview: Human verification | OTP, captcha, login, and verification become explicit needs_user_action states.

name: Content Placeholder 9
text:
Evidence trail
Each run stores screenshots, structured JSON, result history, and artifact links.
textPreview: Evidence trail | Each run stores screenshots, structured JSON, result history, and artifact links.

name: Content Placeholder 10
text:
Secret handling
API keys, cookies, passwords, proxy credentials, and session tokens are masked, hashed, or encrypted.
textPreview: Secret handling | API keys, cookies, passwords, proxy credentials, and session tokens are masked, hashed, or encrypted.

text:
4:40-5:30
What makes CartTruth different from a normal scraper is the safety and evidence layer. It has never-purchase guardrails that block order-finalization and payment-related requests. If Daraz asks for OTP, captcha, login, or manual verification, CartTruth does not bypass it. It marks the job as needing user action. Each run stores screenshots, structured JSON, result history, and redacted artifacts. The output is a verifiable record, not just a scraped number.

name: Footer Placeholder 3
text: CartTruth pitch
textPreview: CartTruth pitch

Why it matters
Checkout totals can vary by IP region, account session, delivery location, and promotion rules.
textPreview: Why it matters | Checkout totals can vary by IP region, account session, delivery location, and promotion rules.

name: Content Placeholder 1

Next controls
Country preference, sticky sessions, rotation, and fallback controls are preview settings until routing support lands.
textPreview: Next controls | Country preference, sticky sessions, rotation, and fallback controls are preview settings until routing support lands.

CartTruth can use a configured TorchProxies ISP or residential profile, with masked credentials and telemetry.
textPreview: Supported now | CartTruth can use a configured TorchProxies ISP or residential profile, with masked credentials and telemetry.

name: Content Placeholder 15

textPreview: Accuracy

textPreview: MVP today

name: Content Placeholder 15
text: Roadmap
textPreview: Roadmap

text:
5:30-6:20
CartTruth also integrates TorchProxies for more realistic checkout verification. This matters because ecommerce pricing and availability can depend on the shopper's network location. CartTruth can route Daraz browser and checkout traffic through a configured TorchProxies ISP or residential profile, while masking proxy credentials. To be accurate about the MVP, advanced controls like requested country preference, sticky sessions, rotation, and fallback are preview settings and part of the routing roadmap.

name: Footer Placeholder 3
text: CartTruth pitch
textPreview: CartTruth pitch

name: Google Shape;533;p58
text: REST and MCP turn CartTruth into infrastructure
textPreview: REST and MCP turn CartTruth into infrastructure

name: Google Shape;534;p58
text: One verifier, three surfaces
textPreview: One verifier, three surfaces

name: TextBox 11
text: Dashboard for humans. REST for systems. MCP for AI agents that need safe checkout evidence without controlling payments or credentials.
textPreview: Dashboard for humans. REST for systems. MCP for AI agents that need safe checkout evidence without controlling payments or credentials.

name: Content Placeholder 10
text:
REST API
Save links, queue jobs, poll status, read runs, and fetch evidence artifacts with scoped ct_ API keys.
textPreview: REST API | Save links, queue jobs, poll status, read runs, and fetch evidence artifacts with scoped ct_ API keys.

name: Content Placeholder 10
text:
MCP server
Agents can list links, queue checks, and read run evidence, but cannot save credentials or drive the browser.
textPreview: MCP server | Agents can list links, queue checks, and read run evidence, but cannot save credentials or drive the browser.

text:
6:20-7:05
CartTruth is not only a dashboard. Users can create scoped API keys for REST, MCP, or both. Through the REST API, systems can add links, queue checks, list jobs, read completed runs, and retrieve evidence. Through MCP, AI tools can use CartTruth as a safe checkout verification tool. This matters because AI agents should not freely improvise checkout browser automation.

name: Footer Placeholder 6

textPreview: Expand by adapter | Recruit 2-3 sellers or commerce teams and add the next checkout adapter from pilot needs.

text:
7:05-7:40
We are starting with a focused beachhead: Daraz Sri Lanka checkout verification. The next milestones are clear: harden the Daraz workflow, complete advanced proxy routing, improve evidence reports, expand adapters, and run pilots with users who already check prices manually.

name: Title 3
text:
CartTruth
Pilot Ask
textPreview: CartTruth | Pilot Ask

name: Subtitle 4
text: Help us validate final-checkout verification with real Daraz users who check prices manually today.
textPreview: Help us validate final-checkout verification with real Daraz users who check prices manually today.

name: Rounded Rectangle 2
text: July 2026
textPreview: July 2026

text:
7:40-8:00
Our ask is simple: help us validate CartTruth with users who regularly need to prove final checkout prices. CartTruth gives them one reliable answer to a messy ecommerce question: what is the real price at checkout?
